children.name: No Unintended Animations or Transitions
children.description: Checks that no animations or transitions were unintentionally added, removed, or modified

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Count animation and transition changes
    animation_changes = (len(ppt_diff.added_animations) + 
                        len(ppt_diff.removed_animations) + 
                        len(ppt_diff.modified_animations))
    
    transition_changes = (len(ppt_diff.added_transitions) + 
                         len(ppt_diff.removed_transitions) + 
                         len(ppt_diff.modified_transitions))
    
    total_changes = animation_changes + transition_changes
    
    if total_changes == 0:
        return "No unintended animation or transition changes detected", 1.0
    else:
        # Small penalty for each unintended change
        penalty = min(1.0, total_changes * 0.1)  # 0.1 penalty per change
        score = max(0.0, 1.0 - penalty)
        return f"Found {total_changes} unintended animation/transition changes (animations: {animation_changes}, transitions: {transition_changes})", score

children.reason: No unintended animation or transition changes detected

The criterion received a perfect score because the evaluation confirmed that no unintended modifications were made to the presentation beyond the intended changes to slide 1. Both sub-criteria performed excellently, with all other slides remaining completely unchanged and no unwanted animations or transitions being added, removed, or modified throughout the presentation. This demonstrates that the modifications were precisely targeted and controlled, maintaining the integrity of the original presentation structure.

root.reason: The criterion received a perfect score because the core task was executed flawlessly - Dr. Sarah Johnson was successfully added to the contributors slide with her correct role as Pediatric Ophthalmologist, exactly as requested. The addition was seamlessly integrated with excellent visual consistency, matching the existing formatting and maintaining the slide's professional appearance. Most importantly, the changes were precisely targeted with no unintended modifications made to other parts of the presentation, demonstrating careful and controlled execution of the task.
